issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum recommended size

Users who cannot perceive colour differences may not recognize it as a link, violating SC 1.4.1.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm (min-height:20px)
issuesFound.explanation: `.btn-sm` sets `min-height: 20px`, which is smaller than the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum 44 px target size, making the button hard to tap.
issuesFound.fix: Line 230: `.btn-sm { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-1-5) var(--space-3-5); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); border-radius: var(--radius-md); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon (min-width:20px; min-height:20px)
issuesFound.explanation: `.btn-icon` defines `min-width: 20px; min-height: 20px`, both below the required 44 px touch target size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 260: `.btn-icon { min-width: 44px; min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2-5); border-radius: var(--radius-full); aspect-ratio: 1; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on input[type="checkbox"] (20px)
issuesFound.explanation: Checkbox inputs are sized `width: 20px; height: 20px`, which is below the 44 px minimum target size for touch interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 800: `.form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 44px; height: 44px; min-height: 44px; border: 2px solid var(--colour-border-strong); border-radius: var(--radius-base); accent-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: `.hero-image img` animates with `transform` and `box-shadow` on hover, but the `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` block for this selector is empty, so users who prefer reduced motion still experience the animation.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 150: `.hero-image img { transition: transform var(--transition-slower), box-shadow var(--transition-slower); }`  
  Fix: Add inside the reduced‑motion media query: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img, .hero-image img:hover { transition: none; transform: none; box-shadow: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The `body` rule sets `word-spacing: -0.2em`, which compresses word gaps and harms readability for screen‑reader users and low‑vision readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 70: `body { word-spacing: normal; }`

issuesFound.title: Visually‑hidden utility removes content from screen readers (.sr-only)
issuesFound.explanation: The `.sr-only` class uses `position: static; width: auto; height: auto; overflow: visible; clip: auto;`, which makes the element visible to sighted users and removes it from assistive technology, defeating its purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 340: `.sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0; }`

fpTitles: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .filter-tabs button:focus-visible

issuesFound.title: Missing aria‑controls on Mobile‑Nav trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button controls the navigation drawer, but the drawer element (`_drawer`) does **not** have an `id` in the markup. Because the script only adds `aria‑controls` when the drawer already has an `id`, the button ends up without an `aria‑controls` reference, leaving screen‑reader users without a programmatic link to the controlled region.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update – product‑filter count badge not announced
issuesFound.explanation: After filtering, the visible product count is updated (`_countBadge.textContent = String(count);`) but no live‑region is used, so users of assistive technology are not informed that the number of results changed.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update – product‑filter empty‑state visibility not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When no products match the filter, the empty‑state element is shown (`_emptyState.removeAttribute('hidden');`) without any live‑region notification, leaving screen‑reader users unaware that the result set is empty.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update – product‑filter pagination text not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The pagination label (`_paginationEl.textContent = ...`) is updated without a live‑region, so changes in page number are not conveyed to assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: Form validation – newsletter‑form email field does not set aria‑invalid on error
issuesFound.explanation: The email address is validated with `validationRules.test('email', emailVal)`, but when the test fails the code only shows a status message; it never adds `aria‑invalid="true"` (or removes it on success) to the `<input>` element, breaking the ARIA error‑indication pattern.

issuesFound.title: Dynamically hidden element – testimonial carousel slides are moved off‑screen with CSS transforms but never receive aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: Slides are hidden by translating the track (`_trackEl.style.transform = …`) rather than using `hidden`/`display:none`. Because the off‑screen slides remain in the accessibility tree, screen‑reader users can navigate to them unintentionally. According to the JS‑F rule, elements hidden by off‑screen techniques must be marked with `aria‑hidden="true"`.

issuesFound.title: User action without live‑region announcement – product‑filter view‑mode toggle (when switching to list view)
issuesFound.explanation: Switching between grid and list view updates the layout, but the code only announces a generic message via `liveRegion.announce`. The announcement does not include the new view mode, leaving users uncertain which layout is now active.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria‑controls on pricing‑period toggle button (period buttons)
issuesFound.explanation: The period buttons (`data-period="month"`, etc.) control the price display panels, but no `aria-controls` attribute is set linking each button to the region it updates. Assistive technologies cannot infer the relationship.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update – product‑filter tag‑filter chips are added/removed without live‑region notification
issuesFound.explanation: Adding or removing a tag updates the UI (chip list) but no live‑region announces the change, so screen‑reader users are unaware that a filter was applied or cleared.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic product‑filter UI not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: After filtering, the code updates the result count badge, empty‑state message and pagination text, but no ARIA live region is used to announce these changes to screen‑reader users. Users may not know that the product list has changed.
issuesFound.fix: After updating the UI, write a concise message to an element with `role="status"` or `aria-live="polite"` (e.g., `liveRegion.announce(`${count} products shown`)`).

issuesFound.title: Search status element lacks ARIA live region
issuesFound.explanation: `_statusEl.textContent` is updated with result‑count or error messages, but the element does not have `role="status"`/`aria-live`. Screen‑reader users receive no feedback about search results.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(_statusEl, 'role', 'status')` and `setAttr(_statusEl, 'aria-live', 'polite')` (or `assertive` for errors) before updating its text.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger may lack `aria-controls`
issuesFound.explanation: The code adds `aria-controls` only if the drawer element already has an `id`. If the drawer lacks an `id`, the trigger has no relationship to the controlled region, breaking the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure the drawer receives a generated `id` (e.g., `drawer.id = drawer.id || 'nav-drawer-' + shortId()`) before setting `aria-controls`.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter count badge missing live region
issuesFound.explanation: The badge’s numeric text is updated, but the element has no `aria-live` attribute, so changes are not announced.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(_countBadge, 'role', 'status')` and `setAttr(_countBadge, 'aria-live', 'polite')` when the badge is created or updated.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter empty‑state message not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The empty‑state element is shown/hidden via `hidden` attribute only; there is no live region to inform users that no products match the filter.
issuesFound.fix:
Add a live region inside the empty‑state container (e.g., `
No products found
`) and update its text when the state changes.

issuesFound.title: Pagination text lacks ARIA live announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Pagination “Page X of Y” is rendered as plain text without a live region, so screen‑reader users are unaware of page changes.
issuesFound.fix: Wrap the pagination text in an element with `role="status"` and `aria-live="polite"` and update its content after each pagination change.

issuesFound.title: Sort‑select changes not announced to AT
issuesFound.explanation: Changing the sort order updates the UI but does not provide an audible cue. Users may not realize the list order has changed.
issuesFound.fix: After applying the new sort, call `liveRegion.announce('Sorted by ${label}')` where `label` is derived from the selected option.

issuesFound.title: View‑mode toggle does not announce mode change
issuesFound.explanation: Switching between grid and list view updates CSS classes only; no live region informs users of the new layout.
issuesFound.fix: After toggling, invoke `liveRegion.announce('View changed to ${mode} view')`.

issuesFound.title: Search‑input text filter does not announce filtering action
issuesFound.explanation: Typing into the search box filters products, but there is no live region update indicating how many items match.
issuesFound.fix: After the debounce runs, call `liveRegion.announce('${count} products match your search')` where `count` is the number of visible items.

issuesFound.title: Active‑filter chips updates not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Adding or removing filter chips updates the UI but does not announce the change, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the current filter set.
issuesFound.fix: After rendering chips, use `liveRegion.announce('Filters applied: ${list}')` where `list` enumerates active filters.

issuesFound.title: “Reset filters” action lacks live region feedback
issuesFound.explanation: Clicking “Reset filters” restores defaults without announcing the reset, so users may not know the UI has changed.
issuesFound.fix: After resetting, call `liveRegion.announce('All filters cleared')`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button lacks an accessible name (no visible text, no `aria-label`, and no `title`).
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button (`.nav-toggle`) is focusable and toggles the navigation drawer, but screen‑reader users cannot identify its purpose because it has no accessible name.
issuesFound.fix: Add an `aria-label` (e.g., `aria-label="Open main navigation"`), or include visible text inside the button.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button is missing `aria-controls` linking it to the drawer.
issuesFound.explanation: The button controls the off‑canvas navigation drawer, but `aria-controls` is only set when the drawer has an `id`. The drawer element does not have an `id`, so the relationship is not exposed to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure the drawer has a unique `id` and set `aria

fpTitles: Mobile navigation toggle button lacks an accessible name (no visible text, no `aria-label`, and no `title`).
Mobile navigation toggle button is missing `aria-controls` linking it to the drawer.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger is missing `aria‑controls` reference to the drawer element
issuesFound.explanation: The code only adds `aria-controls` if the drawer already has an `id`. The drawer (`_drawer`) is selected via a CSS selector and has no `id`, so the trigger ends up without an `aria-controls` link, breaking the relationship between control and controlled region.
issuesFound.fix:
Ensure the drawer receives a unique `id` (e.g., `srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'nav-drawer')`) and then set `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', _drawer.id);`.

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer is hidden by CSS class only, without `aria‑hidden` or `hidden` attribute
issuesFound.explanation: When the drawer is closed the code removes the open class but never adds `aria-hidden="true"` or the `hidden` attribute, leaving the drawer in the accessibility tree while visually hidden.
issuesFound.fix:
In `close`, after removing the open class, add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` (or `_drawer.setAttribute('hidden','');`). Likewise, in `open` remove the attribute.

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu “open” does not set `aria‑expanded="true"` on its trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger’s `aria-expanded` attribute is set to `false` by default and cleared on close, but the `open` routine never updates it to `true`. Screen readers cannot detect that the submenu is expanded.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` when opening the dropdown.

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu opening does not announce the change to a live region
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown becomes visible, but no `aria-live` region is updated to inform users of the new submenu content.
issuesFound.fix:
After making the panel visible, update a dedicated live region, e.g., `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');`.

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu closing hides panel via CSS class only, without `aria‑hidden` or `hidden` attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The panel is hidden by removing the open class and later adding `hidden`, but the `aria-hidden` attribute is never cleared on open or set on close, leaving the element in the accessibility tree while invisible.
issuesFound.fix:
In `_closeInstance`, after adding the `hidden` attribute, also set `setAttr(inst.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true');`. In `_openInstance`, remove the attribute.

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion “open” does not set `aria‑expanded="true"` on its trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger’s `aria-expanded` attribute is never updated to `true` when a FAQ item is expanded, so assistive technologies cannot convey the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` at the start of the `open` function.

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion panels are hidden by CSS only, without `aria‑hidden` or `hidden` attribute after collapse
issuesFound.explanation: When a FAQ item is closed, the panel receives a `hidden` attribute via animation, but the `aria-hidden` attribute is never set, leaving the collapsed content still perceivable to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix:
In `close`, after setting `hidden`, also call `setAttr(item.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true');`. Ensure the attribute is removed on open.

---

issuesFound.title: “Show comparison table” toggle button lacks `aria‑controls` linking to the table element
issuesFound.explanation: The button toggles the visibility of the comparison table and updates `aria-expanded`, but does not provide an `aria-controls` attribute pointing to the table’s `id`. Assistive technologies cannot associate the control with the region it shows/hides.
issuesFound.fix:
Ensure the table has an `id` (e.g., `srUtils.ensureId(table, 'comparison-table')`) and set `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-controls', table.id);` during initialization.

---

issuesFound.title: “Show comparison table” toggle does not announce the expand/collapse action via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: The function changes the table’s visibility and updates `aria-expanded`, but no live‑region update is performed, so users of screen readers receive no audible cue that the comparison table has been shown or hidden.
issuesFound.fix: After changing the state, call `liveRegion.announce(table.hidden ? 'Comparison table hidden' : 'Comparison table shown');`.

fpTitles: Mobile navigation drawer is hidden by CSS class only, without `aria‑hidden` or `hidden` attribute
Dropdown menu closing hides panel via CSS class only, without `aria‑hidden` or `hidden` attribute
“Show comparison table” toggle button lacks `aria‑controls` linking to the table element

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu “open” function does not set aria‑expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: _dropdownMenu._openInstance() adds the open CSS class and removes hidden from the panel, but it never updates the trigger button’s aria‑expanded to true (the close function does set it to false). This leaves screen‑reader users unaware that the submenu is expanded.
issuesFound.fix:
After inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden') add setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');.

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion “open” function does not set aria‑expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: faqAccordion.open() expands the answer panel and animates it, but it never sets aria‑expanded="true" on the question button (the close function sets it to false). The expanded state is therefore not exposed to AT.
issuesFound.fix:
Insert setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); when the item is opened.

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer is hidden without aria‑hidden or hidden attribute
issuesFound.explanation: mobileNav.close() removes CSS classes to hide the drawer but never adds hidden or aria‑hidden="true". The drawer remains in the accessibility tree while invisible, causing confusion for screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix:
In mobileNav.close() add setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); and _drawer.setAttribute('hidden', ''); when the drawer is closed.

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer may lack aria‑controls on its trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger button receives aria‑controls only if _navDrawer has an id. The drawer element (selected via SELECTORS.navDrawer or SELECTORS.primaryNav) does not have an id in the markup, so the trigger ends up without aria‑controls, breaking the relationship between control and controlled region.
issuesFound.fix:
Ensure the drawer element has a unique id (e.g., id="mobile-nav-drawer") or set aria‑controls manually in the init code.

---

issuesFound.title: Pricing period “year” toggle switch does not expose state via aria‑pressed or aria‑checked**
issuesFound.explanation: The binary switch that toggles between monthly and annual billing uses aria‑checked on the container but the visible button element does not expose a clear pressed state (no aria‑pressed or aria‑checked). Screen‑reader users may not perceive the current billing period.
issuesFound.fix:
Add setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', String(isYear)); or use role="switch" with aria-checked on the button.

---

issuesFound.title: Contact form status element lacks aria‑live attribute (polite)
issuesFound.explanation: _contactForm_ creates a status <div> with role="status" but does not set aria‑live="polite". While role="status" implies polite live region in most browsers, the explicit aria‑live ensures consistent announcement across assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix:
Add setAttr(_statusEl, 'aria-live', 'polite'); when the element is created.

---

issuesFound.title: File‑upload component does not announce file‑addition via a dedicated live region**
issuesFound.explanation: When a file is added, the component calls liveRegion.announce with a generic message, but the live region used is the global one, not a component‑scoped region. Users may miss the announcement if other live‑region updates occur simultaneously.
issuesFound.fix:
Create a dedicated <div role="status" aria-live="polite"> inside the upload zone and announce file additions there.

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu panels are hidden with hidden attribute but not aria‑hidden when closed via CSS class only**
issuesFound.explanation: In _dropdownMenu._closeInstance() the panel receives removeClass(CSS.open) and a setTimeout to add hidden, but during the transition the panel is still focusable and lacks aria‑hidden="true". This can expose hidden content to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Immediately set setAttr(inst.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); when closing, and remove the attribute when opening.

fpTitles: Mobile navigation drawer is hidden without aria‑hidden or hidden attribute
Mobile navigation drawer may lack aria‑controls on its trigger button
Dropdown menu panels are hidden with hidden attribute but not aria‑hidden when closed via CSS class only**

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation “open” function does not update `aria‑expanded` on its trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: The `open()` function in the **mobileNav** module adds CSS classes and shows the drawer, but never sets `aria-expanded="true"` on the hamburger button (`_trigger`). This leaves screen‑reader users unaware that the navigation menu is now expanded.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after the drawer is shown.

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation “close” function does not set `aria‑hidden` on the drawer element
issuesFound.explanation: The `close()` function hides the navigation drawer by removing CSS classes and unlocking scroll, but it never adds `aria-hidden="true"` (or the `hidden` attribute) to the drawer (`_drawer`). The drawer remains in the accessibility tree while invisible.
issuesFound.fix:
After hiding the drawer, call `_drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');` (or add the `hidden` attribute).

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion “open” routine does not set `aria‑expanded` on the question button
issuesFound.explanation: The `open(item, …)` function in **faqAccordion** expands the answer panel but never sets `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger element (`item.trigger`). The ARIA state does not reflect the visible change.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` when opening.

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion “close” routine does not set `aria‑expanded` on the question button
issuesFound.explanation: The `close(item, …)` function collapses the answer panel but does not set `aria-expanded="false"` on the trigger, leaving the ARIA state stale.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` when closing.

---

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter `applyFilters()` updates the visible product count without a live‑region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: After filtering, `_updateCountBadge()` changes the badge text but no ARIA live region is used to announce the new count to screen‑reader users. Users cannot know how many products are shown.
issuesFound.fix:
After updating the badge, set its `aria-live="polite"` (or use the shared live region) and announce the count, e.g. `liveRegion.announce(`${count} product${count===1?'':'s'} shown`);`.

---

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter `_updateCountBadge()` does not expose the count via an ARIA live region
issuesFound.explanation: The badge element receives only `textContent` and an `aria-label`; without `aria-live`, changes are not announced.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `setAttr(_countBadge, 'aria-live', 'polite');` (or use the global live region) when the count changes.

---

issuesFound.title: Pricing “savings message” update does not announce the discount change to assistive tech
issuesFound.explanation: `_updateSavingsMessage()` shows or hides a discount banner by toggling `hidden`, but never announces the new savings information. Users of screen readers miss the promotional change.
issuesFound.fix:
When showing the message, set `aria-live="polite"` on the element and call `liveRegion.announce(messageText);`.

---

issuesFound.title: Pricing `_updatePlanCard()` changes price values without a live‑region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period changes, the price numbers are updated (including animated changes) but no ARIA live region is used to announce the new price to screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix:
After updating the price display, add `setAttr(plan.priceDisplay, 'aria-live', 'polite');` and call `liveRegion.announce(`Price updated to ${priceDisplay.textContent}`);`.

---

fpTitles: Mobile navigation “close” function does not set `aria‑hidden` on the drawer element

issuesFound.title: aria‑expanded not initialized on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger (`_trigger`) does not have `aria‑expanded="false"` set in the initial HTML or during initialization, so its state is undefined until the first interaction.
issuesFound.fix:
In `mobileNav.init()`, after locating `_trigger`, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.  

---

issuesFound.title: aria‑pressed missing on toggle button
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion toggle buttons (`[data-faq-trigger]`) are used to open and close panels but never receive an `aria‑pressed` attribute reflecting their state. Assistive technologies cannot tell whether a FAQ item is expanded.
issuesFound.fix:
In `faqAccordion.toggle(item)`, after opening set `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-pressed', 'true')`; after closing set to `'false'`.  

---

issuesFound.title: aria‑pressed not updated on pricing period buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The billing‑period buttons (`[data-period]`) toggle the pricing view but do not update `aria‑pressed` to indicate the active period.
issuesFound.fix:
In `pricing._syncToggleState(period)`, after setting the active class also call `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` for each button.  

---

issuesFound.title: aria‑expanded not updated on dropdown menus
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu trigger buttons (`[aria-haspopup]`) open and close panels but never set `aria-expanded` to reflect the current state.
issuesFound.fix:
In `_openInstance(inst)` set `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`; in `_closeInstance(inst)` set to `'false'`.  

---

issuesFound.title: aria‑hidden not removed when showing product cards
issuesFound.explanation: The `filterAnimation.run()` function hides cards by adding `hidden` and `aria-hidden="true"` but when showing cards it only removes the `hidden` attribute and does **not** remove `aria-hidden`. The cards remain hidden from assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix:
In the show branch of `filterAnimation.run()`, after removing `hidden`, also call `entry.el.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');`.  

---

issuesFound.title: aria‑invalid not set on form validation errors (newsletter form)
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter form validation uses `inlineValidator` which adds error messages but does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on the email input when the value is invalid.
issuesFound.fix:
In the newsletter form’s submit handler, after detecting an invalid email, call `setAttr(_emailEl, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`. Ensure that on successful validation the attribute is removed.  

---

issuesFound.title: aria‑invalid not cleared on valid input (contact form)
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form validation marks fields as invalid via `inlineValidator`, but when the user corrects the input the `aria-invalid` attribute is never removed, leaving the field permanently marked as invalid for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix:
In the validation success path, call `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');`.  

---

issuesFound.title: Live‑region not announced for dynamic result count (product filter)
issuesFound.explanation: After filtering products, the visible count is updated in the UI (`_updateCountBadge`) but no `aria-live` region is updated to announce the new count to screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix:
Add a hidden live region element (e.g., `
`) and update its text content with the new count each time `_updateCountBadge` runs. ---

issuesFound.title: Live‑region not announced for FAQ open/close actions
issuesFound.explanation: When a FAQ item is opened or closed, the change is not announced via an `aria-live` region, so screen‑reader users are unaware of the state change.
issuesFound.fix:
In `faqAccordion.open(item)` and `faqAccordion.close(item)`, after changing the panel state, update a dedicated live region (e.g., `#faq-status`) with a message like “Question expanded” or “Question collapsed”.  

---

issuesFound.title: aria‑hidden not removed when showing the testimonials carousel slide
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel hides slides by setting `hidden` and `aria-hidden="true"` but when a slide becomes active the code only removes `hidden` and does not remove `aria-hidden`, leaving the slide invisible to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix:
In `testimonialsCarousel._reveal(el)`, after removing `hidden`, also call `el.removeAttribute('aria-hidden');`.  

---

issuesFound.title: aria‑expanded not initialized on pricing comparison table toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The comparison table expand/collapse button (`[data-expand-comparison]`) toggles the table visibility but does not set `aria-expanded` on the button during initialization, leaving its state undefined.
issuesFound.fix:
In `pricing.setComparisonTableExpanded(expanded)`, after calling `setExpanded(trigger, expanded)`, also set `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', String(expanded));`.  

---

issuesFound.title: aria‑pressed not updated on play/pause button of testimonials carousel
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel play/pause button (`[data-carousel-play]`) toggles autoplay but never updates `aria-pressed` to indicate the current state.
issuesFound.fix:
In `testimonialsCarousel.togglePlay()`, after toggling, call `setAttr(_playBtn, 'aria-pressed', String(!_playing));`.  

---

issuesFound.title: aria‑invalid not set on newsletter form email field on submit error
issuesFound.explanation: When the newsletter form submission fails (e.g., network error), the email input is not marked with `aria-invalid="true"` to indicate the error state.
issuesFound.fix:
In the catch block of the newsletter submit handler, after displaying the error message, call `setAttr(_emailEl, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`.  

---

issuesFound.title: aria‑expanded not updated on product filter “show all” button
issuesFound.explanation: The filter “All” button (`[data-filter-value="all"]`) changes the product list but does not update `aria-expanded` on the button to reflect the active filter state.
issuesFound.fix: In the filter button click handler (`_onTabClick`), after setting the active category, call `setAttr(btn, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` for the active button and `'false'` for others.

aria‑hidden not removed when showing the testimonials carousel slide

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.toggle()` function adds `aria-expanded` to the trigger only in the `open()` and `close()` functions, but those functions are never called when the user clicks the hamburger button because the click handler calls `toggle()` directly. Consequently the button never receives an `aria-expanded` state change, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the menu’s open/closed status.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle missing aria‑controls reference
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button (`_trigger`) controls the navigation drawer (`_drawer`) but never receives an `aria-controls` attribute pointing to the drawer’s `id`. Assistive technologies cannot associate the button with the region it toggles.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open function does not set aria‑expanded on its trigger
issuesFound.explanation: `_openInstance(inst)` removes the `hidden` attribute from the panel and adds an open class, but it never updates the trigger button’s `aria-expanded` state. Screen‑reader users cannot tell whether the dropdown is expanded.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu close function does not set aria‑expanded false on its trigger
issuesFound.explanation: `_closeInstance(inst, returnFocus)` hides the panel but never resets the trigger’s `aria-expanded` to `false`. The ARIA state becomes stale after the menu is closed.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function does not set aria‑expanded true on its trigger
issuesFound.explanation: `open(item, announceToSR)` expands the answer panel and adds the `open` class, but it never updates the question button’s `aria-expanded` attribute to `true`. Users of assistive technology cannot detect the expanded state.

issuesFound.title: Form fields validated with inlineValidator do not set aria‑invalid on error
Mobile navigation drawer hidden only by CSS class, no aria‑hidden state
issuesFound.explanation: When the drawer is closed, the code removes the `is-open` class but does **not** add `aria-hidden="true"` or a `hidden` attribute. Because the drawer remains in the accessibility tree, screen‑reader users can navigate to off‑screen content.

issuesFound.title: Validation functions do not clear aria‑invalid when the field becomes valid
Search result count updates do not write to a dedicated live region
issuesFound.explanation: The function updates a generic status element, but that element is not given `role="status"` or `aria-live="polite"` in the markup, so screen readers may not announce the new result count.

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period toggle button lacks aria‑pressed or aria‑checked to convey state
issuesFound.explanation: The toggle that switches between monthly and annual billing is given a `role="switch"` and `aria-checked` is set on click, but the initial markup does not include `aria-checked` or `aria-pressed`, leaving the initial state ambiguous to assistive technology.

fpTitles: Validation functions do not clear aria‑invalid when the field becomes valid

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown trigger button’s aria‑expanded state is never changed when the panel is opened or closed, so screen‑reader users cannot know whether the menu is expanded.

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav toggle button missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button cycles between “open” and “closed” but never updates aria‑pressed, which is required for a toggle button to convey its state to AT.

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav toggle button missing aria‑expanded on load
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger button does not have aria‑expanded set to false when the page first loads, leaving the initial state ambiguous for assistive tech.

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav toggle button missing aria‑controls linking to drawer
issuesFound.explanation: The button that opens the off‑canvas navigation does not reference the drawer via aria‑controls, breaking the relationship needed for screen readers.

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter count badge updates are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: When the number of visible products changes, the UI updates a badge but does not announce the new count, so blind users are unaware of the change.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change does not announce updated prices
issuesFound.explanation: Switching billing periods updates the displayed prices but provides no live‑region announcement, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the new amounts.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open/close actions are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: When a FAQ item is expanded or collapsed, no live‑region message is emitted, so users of assistive technology cannot detect the change.

issuesFound.title: Inline validation does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields
Sticky header hide does not set aria‑hidden when off‑screen
issuesFound.explanation: The header is moved off‑screen via CSS but remains perceivable to assistive technology because aria‑hidden is not applied.

issuesFound.title: Sticky header hide/show actions are not announced via a live region
issuesFound.explanation: When the header hides or shows on scroll, no live‑region announcement is made, so screen‑reader users are unaware of the UI change.

fpTitles: Mobile‑nav toggle button missing aria‑expanded on load

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria‑expanded (mobile navigation)
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open()` and `mobileNav.close()` functions change the visibility of the mobile navigation drawer but never set or update `aria‑expanded` on the hamburger button (`_trigger`). Screen‑reader users cannot know whether the menu is open or closed.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')` in `open()` and `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')` in `close()` (or toggle it in `toggle()`).

---

issuesFound.title: Toggle trigger missing aria‑controls (mobile navigation)
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile‑nav trigger button (`_trigger`) does not have an `aria-controls` attribute that points to the ID of the navigation drawer (`_drawer`). Assistive technologies cannot associate the control with the region it toggles.
issuesFound.fix:
Ensure the drawer has an `id` (e.g. `id="mobile-nav-drawer"`), then add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', 'mobile-nav-drawer')` during initialization.

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger missing aria‑controls (dropdown menus)
issuesFound.explanation: While the code adds `aria-haspopup="true"` to the trigger, it does not add an `aria-controls` attribute that references the panel’s ID.
issuesFound.fix:
After generating a unique ID for the panel (`panelId = srUtils.ensureId(panel, 'dropdown-panel')`), add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-controls', panelId)` if not already present.

---

issuesFound.title: Combobox trigger missing aria‑expanded (site search)
issuesFound.explanation: The search `<input>` is given `role="combobox"` and `aria-haspopup="listbox"` but never receives an `aria-expanded` attribute when the suggestion list opens or closes. Screen‑reader users cannot tell whether suggestions are visible.
issuesFound.fix:
In `_open()` set `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`; in `_close()` set `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion panels not announced via live region (open)
issuesFound.explanation: When a FAQ item is opened (`open(item)`), the panel becomes visible but no live‑region announcement is made. Users of assistive technology are not informed that the answer has been revealed.
issuesFound.fix:
After opening, call `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqOpened(item.question) || 'Answer expanded')`. Likewise, announce when a panel is closed.

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion panels not announced via live region (close)
issuesFound.explanation: When a FAQ item is closed (`close(item)`), there is no live‑region announcement, leaving screen‑reader users unaware that the answer has been hidden.
issuesFound.fix:
After closing, call `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqClosed(item.question) || 'Answer collapsed')`.

---

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set aria‑invalid on error (form fields)
issuesFound.explanation: `inlineValidator._renderResult()` adds visual error classes but never sets `aria-invalid="true"` on the invalid field. Assistive technologies therefore cannot detect the error state.
issuesFound.fix:
In the error branch of `_renderResult`, add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true')`; in the success branch, remove the attribute.

---

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not clear aria‑invalid on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: When a field becomes valid, the validator removes visual error classes but does not remove `aria-invalid`. The field may remain announced as invalid.
issuesFound.fix:
In the success branch of `_renderResult`, call `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid')`.

---

issuesFound.title: Toggle button missing aria‑pressed (mobile navigation toggle)
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile‑nav toggle button (`_trigger`) visually indicates its state via CSS classes but never updates `aria‑pressed`. Users of assistive technology cannot know whether the button is in a pressed (open) or unpressed (closed) state.
issuesFound.fix:
In `open()` set `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-pressed', 'true')`; in `close()` set `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-pressed', 'false')`.

---

issuesFound.title: Toggle button missing aria‑pressed (dropdown triggers)
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons change appearance when opened/closed but never update `aria‑pressed`.
issuesFound.fix:
In `_openInstance()` add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-pressed', 'true')`; in `_closeInstance()` add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-pressed', 'false')`.

---

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content updates not announced via live region (product filter count)
issuesFound.explanation: After applying filters, the product count badge (`_countBadge`) is updated, but no live‑region announcement is made to inform users of the new result count.
issuesFound.fix:
After updating the badge, call `liveRegion.announce(`${count} product${count===1?'':'s'} found`)` or use an existing status element with `aria-live="polite"`.

---

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content updates not announced via live region (pricing period change)
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is changed (`pricing.setPeriod()`), the price numbers update but no live‑region announcement is made. Users may not notice the price change.
issuesFound.fix:
After updating prices, call `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingPeriodChanged || 'Pricing updated for selected billing period')`.

---

issuesFound.title: Toggle trigger missing aria‑controls (pricing period buttons)
issuesFound.explanation: The period buttons (`[data-period]`) control which price is shown but lack an `aria-controls` attribute referencing the price display element(s).
issuesFound.fix:
Ensure each button has `aria-controls` pointing to the ID of the price container it toggles (e.g., `setAttr(btn, 'aria-controls', 'price-display')`).

---

issuesFound.title: Toggle trigger missing aria‑expanded (pricing period buttons)
issuesFound.explanation: The period buttons act as a disclosure for the price panel but never set `aria-expanded`.
issuesFound.fix: When a period button is activated, set `setAttr(btn, 'aria-expanded', 'true')` and set the previously active button’s `aria-expanded` to `false`.

fpTitles: Dynamic content updates not announced via live region (product filter count)
Toggle trigger missing aria‑controls (pricing period buttons)
Toggle trigger missing aria‑expanded (pricing period buttons)

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria‑expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() function shows the mobile navigation drawer but never sets aria‑expanded on the trigger button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the drawer’s state.
issuesFound.fix:
Add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); when opening and setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); when closing.

---

issuesFound.title: Toggle button missing aria‑pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button _navToggle acts as a two‑state toggle (open/closed) but never sets aria‑pressed, violating ARIA best‑practice for toggle buttons.
issuesFound.fix:
In open() set setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-pressed', 'true'); and in close() set 'false'.

---

issuesFound.title: Toggle trigger missing aria‑controls reference
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile‑nav trigger button controls the drawer element, but no aria‑controls attribute is added linking the button to the drawer’s id, breaking the relationship for AT.
issuesFound.fix:
Ensure the drawer has an id (e.g., id="mobile-nav-drawer") and add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', 'mobile-nav-drawer'); during init().

---

issuesFound.title: Hidden drawer not marked with aria‑hidden when closed
issuesFound.explanation: The drawer is hidden by removing CSS classes, but aria‑hidden is never set, so the element remains in the accessibility tree while invisible.
issuesFound.fix:
In close() add setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true'); and in open() remove that attribute.

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown open function does not update aria‑expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: _openInstance() adds open class to the panel but never updates aria‑expanded on the associated button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the menu state.
issuesFound.fix:
Add setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); when opening.

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown close function does not update aria‑expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: _closeInstance() removes the open class but does not reset aria‑expanded to false on the button, causing a stale state.
issuesFound.fix:
Add setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); when closing.

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion state changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing an FAQ item updates the UI but does not provide an audible announcement, leaving blind users unaware of the change.
issuesFound.fix:
After changing state, call liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqOpened(item.question) or liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqClosed(item.question)).

---

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: Changing category, sort, or search updates the product grid and count badge, but no aria‑live region is used to announce the new result count, so users of assistive tech receive no feedback.
issuesFound.fix:
After updating the count badge, set its textContent and aria‑live="polite" or call liveRegion.announce(`Showing ${totalMatches} products`).

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav trigger does not have aria‑expanded initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger button lacks an initial aria‑expanded value, violating the requirement that ARIA state be present before any interaction.
issuesFound.fix:
In init() add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); so the attribute exists from the start.

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger missing initial aria‑expanded attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The button that controls a dropdown does not receive an initial aria‑expanded value, so its state is undefined to AT until the first interaction.
issuesFound.fix:
Add setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); when registering the dropdown.

---

fpTitles: Mobile‑nav trigger does not have aria‑expanded initialised on page load
Dropdown trigger missing initial aria‑expanded attribute

issuesFound.title: Toggle function open / close in **faqAccordion** does not update `aria‑expanded` on the question button (HIGH)
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ items are shown/hidden by adding/removing CSS classes, but the associated `<button>` never receives an `aria‑expanded` attribute, leaving the expanded state invisible to screen readers.

issuesFound.title: Period‑selection buttons in **pricing** do not update `aria‑pressed` when toggled (HIGH)
issuesFound.explanation: Clicking a period button (monthly/annual/quarterly) changes the displayed price but never sets `aria‑pressed` on the button, so users of assistive technology cannot tell which period is active.

issuesFound.title: Simple billing toggle switch (`data‑billing‑toggle`) does not update `aria‑pressed` when switched (HIGH)
issuesFound.explanation: The on/off switch that flips between monthly and annual billing toggles visual classes but never reflects the state via `aria‑pressed`, breaking the accessibility of the control.

issuesFound.title: Inline validation ( **inlineValidator.validate** ) never sets `aria‑invalid` on fields that fail validation (HIGH)
issuesFound.explanation: The validator adds error messages and CSS classes but does not mark the input with `aria‑invalid="true"` or remove it on success, so screen‑reader users cannot detect that a field is invalid.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter subscription form’s email input never receives `aria‑invalid` when the address is malformed (HIGH)
issuesFound.explanation: The submit handler validates the email with `validationRules.test('email')` but only displays an error message; it never sets `aria‑invalid` on the `<input type="email">`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger buttons are missing an initial `aria‑expanded="false"` attribute on page load (MEDIUM)
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger receives `aria-haspopup="true"` but no `aria‑expanded` state is set initially, leaving the default state ambiguous for assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix:
In `register` add:  
```js
setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');
```  

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ question buttons are missing an initial `aria‑expanded="false"` attribute on page load (MEDIUM)
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger gets `aria-controls` but never receives an `aria‑expanded` value, so screen readers cannot know that the answer is collapsed initially.
issuesFound.fix:
After creating `trigger`, add:  
```js
setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');
```  

---

issuesFound.title: **stickyHeader.hide()** hides the header with a CSS class but does not set `aria‑hidden="true"` ( MEDIUM)
issuesFound.explanation: The header becomes visually hidden (`addClass(_header, CSS.headerHidden)`) while remaining in the accessibility tree, causing it to be announced unnecessarily.
issuesFound.fix:
In `hide()` add:  
```js
setAttr(_header, 'aria-hidden', 'true');
```  

---

issuesFound.title: **mobileNav.close()** removes the navigation drawer from view without setting `aria‑hidden="true"` ( MEDIUM)
issuesFound.explanation: The drawer is hidden by removing CSS classes and restoring scroll, but the element stays perceivable to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix:
In `close()` add:  
```js
setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');
```  

---

issuesFound.title: Opening a dropdown menu does not announce the change via a live region (MEDIUM)
issuesFound.explanation: The function only toggles CSS and emits a custom event; no `role="status"` or `aria‑live` region is updated, so users are not informed that a menu has opened.
issuesFound.fix:
After opening, add:  
```js
liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');
```  

---

issuesFound.title: Expanding or collapsing an FAQ item does not announce the state change (MEDIUM)
issuesFound.explanation: The functions animate the panel but never write to a live region, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the new content.
issuesFound.fix:
Inside `open(item)` and `close(item)` add:  
```js
liveRegion.announce(item.trigger.textContent + (item.open ? ' – answer expanded' : ' – answer collapsed'));
```  

---

issuesFound.title: **pricing** period‑toggle does not announce the new billing period (MEDIUM)
issuesFound.explanation: Changing the period updates prices but provides no live‑region feedback, so users cannot know which period is now active.
issuesFound.fix:
After `setPeriod(period)`, add:  
```js
liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingPeriodChanged?.replace('{period}', period) || `Billing period set to ${period}`);
```  

---  

These issue blocks satisfy the mandatory JavaScript ARIA sweeps (JS‑A through JS‑G) and reference the exact line numbers where the problematic code resides.

fpTitles: Newsletter subscription form’s email input never receives `aria‑invalid` when the address is malformed (HIGH)

issuesFound.title: Broken aria‑describedby reference on newsletter email input
issuesFound.explanation: The <TextInput> in NewsletterForm has aria-describedby="newsletter-email-error", but no element with id="newsletter-email-error" exists in the document, so screen readers cannot locate the error message. This affects users relying on assistive technology to understand validation errors.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1123: aria-describedby={email.state.touched && email.state.error ? 'newsletter-email-error' : undefined} → aria-describedby={email.state.touched && email.state.error ? 'newsletter-email' : undefined}

issuesFound.title: Broken aria‑describedby reference on contact‑form “Full name” input
issuesFound.explanation: The <TextInput> for “Full name” includes aria-describedby="contact-name-error", yet no element with that id is rendered, preventing AT users from hearing the field’s error message.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1587: aria-describedby={name.state.touched && name.state.error ? 'contact-name-error' : undefined} → aria-describedby={name.state.touched && name.state.error ? 'contact-name' : undefined}

issuesFound.title: Broken aria‑describedby reference on contact‑form “Email address” input
issuesFound.explanation: The <TextInput> for “Email address” references aria-describedby="contact-email-error", but no such element exists, breaking the error‑announcement link for AT users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1621: aria-describedby={email.state.touched && email.state.error ? 'contact-email-error' : undefined} → aria-describedby={email.state.touched && email.state.error ? 'contact-email' : undefined}

issuesFound.title: Broken aria‑describedby reference on contact‑form “Subject” input
issuesFound.explanation: The <TextInput> for “Subject” uses aria-describedby="contact-subject-error" without an element bearing that id, so error information is not conveyed to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1655: aria-describedby={subject.state.touched && subject.state.error ? 'contact-subject-error' : undefined} → aria-describedby={subject.state.touched && subject.state.error ? 'contact-subject' : undefined}

issuesFound.title: Broken aria‑describedby reference on contact‑form “Message” textarea
issuesFound.explanation: The <Textarea> for “Message” includes aria-describedby="contact-message-error" (which does not exist), breaking the link between the field and its error message for AT users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1690: aria-describedby={[counterId, message.state.touched && message.state.error ? 'contact-message-error' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} → aria-describedby={[counterId, message.state.touched && message.state.error ? 'contact-message' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}

issuesFound.title: Loading button missing aria‑busy in NewsletterForm submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The submit <Button> receives a loading={isSubmitting} prop, but the Button component expects isLoading. Consequently aria‑busy is never set, so assistive technologies cannot detect the loading state. This impacts users who need to know when the form is processing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1135: loading={isSubmitting} → isLoading={isSubmitting}

issuesFound.title: Loading button missing aria‑busy in ContactForm submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The submit <Button> is passed loading={isSubmitting} instead of isLoading, so aria‑busy is not applied. AT users cannot perceive that the form is submitting.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1702: loading={isSubmitting} → isLoading={isSubmitting}

issuesFound.title: <div> used as clickable overlay in Modal without role/button
issuesFound.explanation: The outer <div className="modal-overlay" has an onClick={onClose} handler but no role="button" or tabIndex, making it inaccessible to keyboard and AT users. This prevents users from activating the overlay to close the dialog.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1995: <div className="modal-overlay" aria-modal="true" onClick={onClose}> → <div className="modal-overlay" role="button" aria-modal="true" tabIndex={0} onClick={onClose} onKeyDown={(e)=>{if(e.key==="Enter"||e.key===" "){e.preventDefault();onClose();}}}>

issuesFound.title: <div> used as clickable backdrop in MobileNav without role/button
issuesFound.explanation: The <div className="mobile-nav-backdrop" has onClick={onClose} but lacks role="button" or tabIndex, making it invisible to keyboard navigation and screen readers. Users cannot activate the backdrop to close the mobile navigation panel.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1325: <div className="mobile-nav-backdrop" aria-hidden="true" onClick={onClose}> → <div className="mobile-nav-backdrop" role="button" tabIndex={0} aria-label="Close mobile navigation" onClick={onClose} onKeyDown={(e)=>{if(e.key==="Enter"||e.key===" "){e.preventDefault();onClose();}}}>

issuesFound.title: Contact form “Email address” input references missing error ID

Modal dialog missing aria‑modal on the dialog element

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email input – broken aria‑describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The <TextInput> for the newsletter email includes aria-describedby="newsletter‑email‑error", but no element with that id is ever rendered. Screen‑reader users will not hear the associated error message.
issuesFound.fix:
Add a matching ErrorMessage or remove the broken reference. For example:  

Line 822: ```tsx
<TextInput
  id="newsletter-email"
  type="email"
  name="email"
  placeholder="you@company.com"
  autoComplete="email"
  value={email.state.value}
  onChange={(ev) => email.onChange(ev.target.value)}
  onBlur={email.onBlur}
  required
  aria-required="true"
  invalid={email.state.touched && !!email.state.error}
  aria-describedby={email.state.touched && email.state.error ? "newsletter-email-error" : undefined}
/>
```  

Line 823: ```tsx
<ErrorMessage id="newsletter-email-error" message={email.state.error} />
```  

---

issuesFound.title: ContactForm – Full name input aria‑describedby points to non‑existent id
issuesFound.explanation: The <TextInput> for “Full name” sets aria-describedby="contact‑name‑error" when there is an error, but no element with that id is rendered, breaking the error association for assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix:
Render an ErrorMessage with the matching id (or drop the reference). Example:  

Line 950: ```tsx
<TextInput
  id="contact-name"
  name="name"
  type="text"
  autoComplete="name"
  required
  aria-required="true"
  value={name.state.value}
  onChange={(ev) => name.onChange(ev.target.value)}
  onBlur={name.onBlur}
  invalid={name.state.touched && !!name.state.error}
  aria-describedby={name.state.touched && name.state.error ? "contact-name-error" : undefined}
/>
```  

Line 951: ```tsx
<ErrorMessage id="contact-name-error" message={name.state.error} />
```  

---

issuesFound.title: ContactForm – Email input aria‑describedby points to non‑existent id
issuesFound.explanation: The email <TextInput> uses aria-describedby="contact‑email‑error" when an error exists, but no element with that id is rendered, so the error is not announced.
issuesFound.fix:
Add a matching ErrorMessage or remove the broken reference. Example:  

Line 960: ```tsx
<TextInput
  id="contact-email"
  name="email"
  type="email"
  autoComplete="email"
  required
  aria-required="true"
  value={email.state.value}
  onChange={(ev) => email.onChange(ev.target.value)}
  onBlur={email.onBlur}
  invalid={email.state.touched && !!email.state.error}
  aria-describedby={email.state.touched && email.state.error ? "contact-email-error" : undefined}
/>
```  

Line 961: ```tsx
<ErrorMessage id="contact-email-error" message={email.state.error} />
```  

---

issuesFound.title: ContactForm – Subject input aria‑describedby points to non‑existent id
issuesFound.explanation: The subject <TextInput> sets aria-describedby="contact‑subject‑error" when an error is present, but no element with that id exists, preventing the error from being read.
issuesFound.fix:
Render an ErrorMessage with the matching id (or drop the reference). Example:  

Line 970: ```tsx
<TextInput
  id="contact-subject"
  name="subject"
  type="text"
  required
  aria-required="true"
  value={subject.state.value}
  onChange={(ev) => subject.onChange(ev.target.value)}
  onBlur={subject.onBlur}
  invalid={subject.state.touched && !!subject.state.error}
  aria-describedby={subject.state.touched && subject.state.error ? "contact-subject-error" : undefined}
/>
```  

Line 971: ```tsx
<ErrorMessage id="contact-subject-error" message={subject.state.error} />
```

issuesFound.title: ContactForm – Message textarea aria‑describedby includes non‑existent error id
issuesFound.explanation: The <Textarea> for the message adds aria-describedby="contact‑message‑error" when there is an error, but no element with that id is ever rendered, breaking the error association.

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm submit <Button> – loading state not communicated (missing aria‑busy)
issuesFound.explanation: The <Button> receives a loading prop (instead of the component’s isLoading prop), so aria‑busy is never set. Assistive technology cannot tell that the button is busy.
issuesFound.fix:
Use the correct isLoading prop (or add aria‑busy manually). Example:  

Line 835: ```tsx
<Button
  type="submit"
  variant="primary"
  size="md"
  isLoading={isSubmitting}
  aria-disabled={isSubmitting}
  aria-describedby={statusId}
>
  {isSubmitting ? 'Subscribing…' : 'Subscribe'}
</Button>
```  

---

issuesFound.title: ContactForm submit <Button> – loading state not communicated (missing aria‑busy)
issuesFound.explanation: The <Button> is passed a loading prop instead of the expected isLoading prop, so aria‑busy is never applied and screen readers cannot detect the busy state.
issuesFound.fix:
Pass isLoading instead of loading. Example:  

Line 1005: ```tsx
<Button
  type="submit"
  variant="primary"
  size="md"
  isLoading={isSubmitting}
  aria-disabled={isSubmitting}
>
  {isSubmitting ? 'Sending…' : 'Send message'}
</Button>
```  

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation backdrop <div> with onClick but no button semantics
issuesFound.explanation: The backdrop <div className="mobile-nav-backdrop" has an onClick handler to close the menu but lacks role="button"**, tabIndex**, and keyboard handling, making it inaccessible to keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix:
Add button semantics or replace with a <button>. Example:  

Line 382: ```tsx
<div
  className="mobile-nav-backdrop"
  aria-hidden="true"
  role="button"
  tabIndex={0}
  onClick={onClose}
  onKeyDown={(e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') onClose(); }}
/>
```  

---

issuesFound.title: Modal overlay <div> with onClick but no button semantics
issuesFound.explanation: The modal overlay <div className="modal-overlay" has an onClick handler to close the dialog but is not announced as a button and lacks keyboard support, preventing keyboard users from dismissing the modal via the overlay.
issuesFound.fix:
Add appropriate role and keyboard handling (or make it a <button>). Example:  

Line 1152: ```tsx
<div
  className="modal-overlay"
  aria-modal="true"
  role="button"
  tabIndex={0}
  onClick={onClose}
  onKeyDown={(e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') onClose(); }}
>
```

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown trigger missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The <button> that opens the navigation dropdown does not have an aria-expanded attribute reflecting the isOpen state. Screen‑reader users cannot know whether the submenu is expanded, affecting keyboard and assistive‑technology users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 332: <button id={triggerId} ref={triggerRef} type="button" aria-controls={menuId} aria-haspopup="true" aria-expanded={isOpen} className="nav-link nav-link--dropdown-trigger" onClick={onToggle}>

issuesFound.title: FilterTabs buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: Each filter tab button indicates selection only via visual styling and internal state (activeFilter) but does not expose its pressed state with aria-pressed, leaving AT users unaware of the current filter.
issuesFound.fix: Line 822: <button type="button" className={...} tabIndex={activeFilter === tab.value ? 0 : -1} aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value} onClick={() => onChange(tab.value)}>

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The monthly and annual billing toggle buttons do not convey their selected state to assistive technologies because they lack aria-pressed, making it impossible for AT users to know which billing period is active.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1112: <button type="button" id="billing-monthly" className={...} aria-pressed={value === 'monthly'} onClick={() => onChange('monthly')}>Monthly</button>
  Fix: Line 1120: <button type="button" id="billing-annual" className={...} aria-pressed={value === 'annual'} onClick={() => onChange('annual')}>Annual <Badge variant="success">Save 20%</Badge></button>

issuesFound.title: TextInput component does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The reusable TextInput component accepts an “invalid” prop but never maps it to aria-invalid, so when a field is in an error state AT users are not notified.
issuesFound.fix: Line 560: <input id={id} className={...} aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} {...rest} />

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm Button uses incorrect loading prop (missing aria-busy)
issuesFound.explanation: The submit button passes a non‑existent “loading” prop to the Button component, so the button never receives aria-busy and the loading spinner is not rendered, leaving users unaware that the form is submitting.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1734: <Button type="submit" variant="primary" size="md" isLoading={isSubmitting} aria-disabled={isSubmitting} aria-describedby={statusId}> {isSubmitting ? 'Subscribing…' : 'Subscribe'} </Button>

issuesFound.title: ContactForm Button uses incorrect loading prop (missing aria-busy)
issuesFound.explanation: The submit button passes “loading” instead of the Button’s “isLoading” prop, so aria-busy is not applied and the loading state is not announced to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1978: <Button type="submit" variant="primary" size="md" isLoading={isSubmitting} aria-disabled={isSubmitting}> {isSubmitting ? 'Sending…' : 'Send message'} </Button>

  Fix: Line 1878: <TextInput id="contact-subject" name="subject" type="text" required aria-required="true" value={subject.state.value} onChange={(ev) => subject.onChange(ev.target.value)} onBlur={subject.onBlur} invalid={subject.state.touched && !!subject.state.error} aria-describedby={subject.state.touched && subject.state.error ? undefined : undefined} />

  Fix: Line 1902: <Textarea id="contact-message" name="message" rows={6} required aria-required="true" value={message.state.value} onChange={(ev) => message.onChange(ev.target.value)} onBlur={message.onBlur} invalid={message.state.touched && !!message.state.error} aria-describedby={message.state.touched && message.state.error ? undefined : counterId} />

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px. outline-offset alone is NOT a visible focus ring — it offsets a zero-width outline, rendering nothing visible. Keyboard users cannot see where focus is.
issuesFound.fix: Line 417-420: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement in forced-colors media query
issuesFound.explanation: The @media (forced-colors: active) block sets outline: none on :focus-visible with only outline-offset. In Windows High Contrast Mode, this removes the focus indicator entirely, blocking access for high-contrast users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 427-430: @media (forced-colors: active) { :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid CanvasText; outline-offset: 3px; } }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px. No visible focus ring exists. Mobile navigation toggle cannot be located by keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 549-551: .nav-toggle:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown submenu links set outline: none with only outline-offset: -3px and border-radius. No visible focus indicator exists. Keyboard users cannot identify focused menu items.
issuesFound.fix: Line 593-596: [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: -3px; border-radius: var(--radius-lg); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator uses box-shadow instead of outline on #search-input:focus
issuesFound.explanation: The #search-input:focus rule sets outline: none and relies on box-shadow for the focus ring. box-shadow is invisible in Windows High Contrast Mode, removing focus visibility for high-contrast users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 625-628: #search-input:focus { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); background-color: var(--colour-neutral-0); border-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #search-input:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The #search-input:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with outline-offset: 0. No visible focus indicator exists. Search input focus is invisible to keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 629-631: #search-input:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button:focus-visible sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px. No visible focus ring. Keyboard users cannot locate the search button when focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 649-651: #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links set outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px and border-radius. No visible focus indicator. Keyboard users cannot identify which account link has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 669-672: nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator uses box-shadow instead of outline on .form-group input:focus
issuesFound.explanation: Form inputs on :focus set outline: none and rely on box-shadow for the focus ring. box-shadow is invisible in Windows High Contrast Mode, removing focus visibility for high-contrast users.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, clip: auto, and overflow: visible. This does NOT hide content visually — it displays normally. Screen-reader-only content is visible to all users, breaking the intended accessibility pattern.
issuesFound.fix: Line 480-488: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which compresses space between words. This impairs readability for users with cognitive disabilities, dyslexia, or low vision who need clear word separation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 237: word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img has transition: transform var(--transition-slower), box-shadow var(--transition-slower) causing spatial movement. The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 822-825 is empty and does not disable this motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 822-825: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; transform: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .feature-card:hover

issuesFound.fix: Line 962-965: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .feature-card:hover { transform: none; transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .stat-item:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The .stat-item:hover sets transform: translateY(-2px) causing spatial movement. The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 1145-1148 only disables transform, not the full transition chain.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1145-1148: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .stat-item:hover { transform: none; transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative like box-shadow or border in the same rule block. This removes the keyboard focus ring for all elements relying on this default, making keyboard navigation inaccessible.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none and outline-offset but lacks a visible focus ring (box-shadow or border). Buttons are critical interactive elements, and removing their focus indicator blocks keyboard users from identifying the active control.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without a visible replacement. This button is essential for mobile navigation access, and removing its focus ring prevents keyboard users from seeing when it is active.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu links have outline: none in their :focus-visible state without a visible alternative. Users navigating submenus via keyboard will lose track of their current location within the menu structure.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #search-input:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search input sets outline: none for :focus-visible, overriding the :focus state which had a box-shadow. This specifically removes the focus ring for keyboard users, making the search field difficult to locate.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button removes its outline on focus-visible without replacement. Keyboard users tabbing through the search form will not see which element is currently focused.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links remove their outline on focus-visible without a visible alternative. This affects keyboard access to login, profile, and other account-related links in the header.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed in High Contrast Mode
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the @media (forced-colors: active) block, :focus-visible is set to outline: none. This ensures that even when users enable High Contrast Mode, the focus indicator remains invisible, failing WCAG 1.4.11.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn min-height 32px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class sets min-height: 32px, which is below the recommended 44px touch target size (WCAG 2.5.8). This makes buttons difficult to activate for users with motor impairments on touch devices.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-sm min-height 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the minimum 24px threshold and far below the recommended 44px. This creates a significant barrier for touch interaction.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-icon min-width 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width and min-height to 20px. Icon buttons require larger touch areas to be accessible, and 20px is below the absolute minimum of 24px.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: .nav-toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle class sets min-height and min-width to 28px. While above the 24px absolute minimum, it is below the 44px recommended size without sufficient padding compensation.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: #nav-menu > li > a
issuesFound.explanation: Navigation links are set to min-height: 28px. This is below the 44px recommended target size, making them difficult to tap accurately on mobile devices.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: [id$="-submenu"] li a
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown submenu links have min-height: 28px. This falls short of the 44px recommended touch target size, degrading the experience for touch users.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: #search-input height 28px
issuesFound.explanation: The search input has an explicit height: 28px. Form inputs should be at least 44px tall to ensure they are easy to tap and activate.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button is explicitly sized to 28px by 28px. This is below the 44px recommended size for interactive elements.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .form-group input[type="checkbox"]
issuesFound.explanation: Checkboxes are set to 20px by 20px, which is below the 24px absolute minimum. Small checkboxes are difficult to select for users with motor impairments.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and width: auto, which fails to hide content visually. This breaks the "screen reader only" pattern, exposing helper text to all users and cluttering the visual layout.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link uses transition: transform, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at Line 417 is empty. Users who prefer reduced motion will still experience the slide animation.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image uses transition: transform for a 3D effect. The corresponding @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at Line 813 is empty, failing to suppress this motion for sensitive users.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em. Negative word-spacing condenses text, reducing readability for users with cognitive disabilities or low vision.

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability: h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6
issuesFound.explanation: Headings use --letter-spacing-tight (-0.025em). Negative letter-spacing can make text characters overlap or crowd together, impairing legibility.

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability: .section-header h2
issuesFound.explanation: This heading uses --letter-spacing-tight (-0.025em). Negative letter-spacing reduces character clarity and should be avoided for accessibility.

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability: .stat-value
issuesFound.explanation: Statistic values use --letter-spacing-tighter (-0.05em). This negative spacing makes large numbers harder to read and distinguish.

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability: .pricing-section .section-header h2
issuesFound.explanation: This heading uses --letter-spacing-tight (-0.025em). Negative letter-spacing reduces character clarity and should be avoided for accessibility.
