issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .section-header p foreground #aaaaaa / background #ffffff
issuesFound.explanation: The paragraph text uses --colour-text-secondary (#aaaaaa) on a white background (#ffffff). The contrast ratio is approximately 1.87:1, which fails the WCAG 4.5:1 requirement for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .feature-card p foreground #aaaaaa / background #ffffff
issuesFound.explanation: Card body text uses --colour-text-secondary (#aaaaaa) on a white background. This low contrast makes the text difficult to read for users with low vision.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .step-content p foreground #aaaaaa / background #ffffff
issuesFound.explanation: Step description text uses --colour-text-secondary (#aaaaaa) on a white background. This fails WCAG AA contrast requirements.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .product-body > p:not(.product-tagline) foreground #aaaaaa / background #ffffff
issuesFound.explanation: Product description text uses --colour-text-secondary (#aaaaaa) on a white background. This fails WCAG AA contrast requirements.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .plan-tagline foreground #aaaaaa / background #ffffff
issuesFound.explanation: Pricing tagline text uses --colour-text-secondary (#aaaaaa) on a white background. This fails WCAG AA contrast requirements.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .testimonial-card figcaption p foreground #aaaaaa / background #ffffff
issuesFound.explanation: Testimonial metadata text uses --colour-text-secondary (#aaaaaa) on a white background. This fails WCAG AA contrast requirements.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links have text-decoration: none and rely solely on color change on hover to indicate interactivity. Users with color blindness may not perceive these as links.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links: .social-links a
issuesFound.explanation: Social media links have text-decoration: none and rely solely on color change on hover. Non-color visual cues like underlines are required to identify links.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible rule sets outline: none with only outline-offset. outline-offset on a zero-width outline renders nothing, removing the focus ring for keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 433: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on @media (forced-colors: active) :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Inside forced-colors mode, the focus indicator is explicitly set to outline: none, making it invisible for high-contrast users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 445: @media (forced-colors: active) { :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid CanvasText; outline-offset: 3px; } }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-brand:focus-visible

issuesFound.fix: Line 458: .focus-ring-brand:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em. Negative word-spacing reduces space between words, impairing readability for users with cognitive disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 233: body { word-spacing: 0; }

issuesFound.title: pricing comparison trigger missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The function `setComparisonTableExpanded` correctly updates `aria-expanded` on the trigger `[data-expand-comparison]`, but the `init()` function does not locate this trigger to set the initial `aria-expanded="false"` state. If the table is hidden on page load, the trigger button lacks the initial ARIA state, causing screen readers to misreport the control's status until it is interacted with.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1935: const comparisonTrigger = qs('[data-expand-comparison]'); if (comparisonTrigger) { setAttr(comparisonTrigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false'); setAttr(comparisonTrigger, 'aria-controls', 'pricing-comparison'); }

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close actions not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance` and `_closeInstance` functions toggle the visibility of dropdown panels and update `aria-expanded` on the trigger, but they do not announce the state change to screen readers via a live region. Users relying on assistive technology are not informed when a menu opens or closes, unlike other modules (e.g., mobileNav, faqAccordion) that use `liveRegion.announce`.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1335: emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:open', { trigger: inst.trigger, panel: inst.panel }); liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');
  Line 1350: emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:close', { trigger: inst.trigger, panel: inst.panel }); liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent banner actions not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `_showBanner`, `_onAccept`, and `_onDecline` functions change the visibility and state of the cookie consent banner but do not announce these changes via `liveRegion`. Users are not informed when the banner appears or when their consent choice is processed, creating a gap in feedback compared to other interactive modules.

issuesFound.title: discountCodeInput invalid state not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onApply` function validates the discount code and announces errors via `liveRegion`, but it fails to set `aria-invalid="true"` on the input element `_inputEl` when the code is invalid. This prevents screen readers from identifying the field as erroneous programmatically, relying solely on the announcement which may be missed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2185: if (result.valid) { ... } else { _inputEl.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); liveRegion.announceAssertive(result.message || 'Invalid code'); }

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer visibility not managed with aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The `_drawer` element's visibility is toggled via CSS class `is-open` without explicit `aria-hidden` management. If the CSS uses `transform` or `visibility` instead of `display: none` for the closed state, the drawer remains in the accessibility tree while visually hidden. Explicit `aria-hidden` toggling ensures the drawer is removed from the accessibility tree when closed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1095: addClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open); _drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'false');
  Line 1115: removeClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open); _drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');

issuesFound.title: Use of deprecated aria-dropeffect attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The fileUploadField.bind function sets the aria-dropeffect attribute to 'none' and 'copy' on the drop zone element. This attribute was deprecated in WAI-ARIA 1.1 and is no longer supported by browsers or assistive technologies. Its use creates noise in the accessibility tree without providing functional benefit.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3363: removeAttr(zoneEl, 'aria-dropeffect');
Line 3368: removeAttr(zoneEl, 'aria-dropeffect');

issuesFound.title: Contradictory ARIA roles and live region politeness
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator._getOrCreateErrorEl function creates an error message element with role="alert" and explicitly sets aria-live="polite". The role="alert" implicitly defines an assertive live region. Explicitly setting it to polite contradicts the role's implicit behavior and may cause inconsistent announcements across different screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2937: const errorEl = createElement('span', { id: existingId, class: 'field-error', role: 'alert', 'aria-atomic': 'true' });

issuesFound.title: aria-roledescription used without valid base role
issuesFound.explanation: The testimonialsCarousel.init function sets aria-roledescription="carousel" on the root element and aria-roledescription="slide" on slide elements without ensuring they have a valid base role (e.g., role="region" for the carousel). Additionally, 'carousel' and 'slide' are not standard ARIA roles; using them as descriptions on generic elements (like divs) renders the attribute ineffective for assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2531: if (!_rootEl.getAttribute('role')) _rootEl.setAttribute('role', 'region'); _rootEl.setAttribute('aria-roledescription', 'Carousel of customer testimonials');
Line 2539: el.setAttribute('role', 'group'); el.setAttribute('aria-roledescription', 'Testimonial slide');

issuesFound.title: Table accessible name not enforced by utility
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.makeTableAccessible function checks for a <caption> or aria-label/aria-labelledby on the table but only logs a debug message if they are missing. It does not programmatically add an accessible name. Data tables without an accessible name fail WCAG 1.3.1 (Info and Relationships) and 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value).
issuesFound.fix: Line 1217: if (!label) { const caption = createElement('caption'); caption.textContent = 'Data Table'; table.insertBefore(caption, table.firstChild); }

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav aria-expanded state may desync on load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init function only sets aria-expanded="false" on the trigger if the attribute is missing. If the HTML contains aria-expanded="true" (e.g., from SSR) but the visual state is closed (no CSS class), the JavaScript state (_open = false) will not correct the attribute. This causes a mismatch where screen readers announce the menu as open while it appears closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1453: const isOpen = _drawer && _drawer.classList.contains(CONFIG.navigation.navOpenClass || CSS.open); setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', String(isOpen));

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size — checkbox input
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input has an explicit width and height of 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8. Users with motor impairments may struggle to activate the small checkbox accurately on touch devices.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1115: `.form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; ... }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size — search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has an explicit width and height of 28px (and padding: 0), placing it in the 24–43px range without sufficient compensating padding to reach the 44px recommended target size. This makes activation difficult for users with tremors or limited dexterity.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~655: `#site-search button[type="submit"] { width: 44px; height: 44px; ... }` or add `padding: 8px;` to achieve 44px effective size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size — small button variant
issuesFound.explanation: The `.btn-sm` class sets a min-height of 36px with only 6px vertical padding (space-1-5), resulting in a touch target between 24–43px without adequate padding compensation to meet the 44px recommended size.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~720: `.btn-sm { min-height: 44px; ... }` or increase vertical padding to at least 8px.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size — footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links use `display: inline-block` with `min-height: 24px` and no compensating padding, placing them at the minimum 24px threshold without meeting the 44px recommended target size for comfortable activation.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1093: `footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { min-height: 44px; padding-block: var(--space-2); ... }`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast — stat label text
issuesFound.explanation: The `.stat-label` uses `color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.75)` (75% white) against the brand primary blue background (#005fcc), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 4.2:1, which fails the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text (14px, non-bold) under WCAG 1.4.3.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~945: `.stat-label { color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.9); ... }` (increase opacity to at least 0.9) or use `#ffffff` with a subtle text-shadow.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast — newsletter paragraph text
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter section paragraph uses `color: rgba(255,255,255,0.8)` against the gradient background containing #005fcc, yielding a contrast ratio of approximately 4.35:1, failing the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text under WCAG 1.4.3.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1057: `.newsletter-section > p { color: rgba(255,255,255,0.95); ... }`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast — newsletter input hint
issuesFound.explanation: The `.input-hint` within the newsletter section uses `color: rgba(255,255,255,0.65)`, which results in a contrast ratio below 4.5:1 against the blue gradient background, making the hint text difficult to read for users with low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1085: `.newsletter-section .input-hint { color: rgba(255,255,255,0.9); ... }`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast — pricing toggle badge
issuesFound.explanation: The badge inside the active pricing toggle button uses `background-color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.25)` with `color: var(--colour-neutral-0)` (#ffffff), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 3.65:1 against the effective light-blue background, failing the 4.5:1 requirement for small bold text (12px, weight 600).
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1020: `.pricing-toggle button[aria-pressed="true"] .badge { background-color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.4); color: #000000; ... }` or use a solid background colour.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative — account navigation
issuesFound.explanation: Links within the account navigation have `text-decoration: none` and rely solely on colour change (from neutral-700 to brand-primary) for identification, with no non-colour visual differentiator (such as underline, border-bottom, or font-weight change) at rest, violating WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~673: `nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a { text-decoration: underline; }` or add `border-bottom: 1px solid currentColor;` or `font-weight: var(--font-weight-bold);` to distinguish links from surrounding text.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative — footer address links
issuesFound.explanation: Address links in the footer brand section use `text-decoration: none` with only a colour change on hover, lacking a non-colour visual differentiator at rest to distinguish them from the surrounding address text, failing WCAG 1.4.1.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1070: `.footer-brand address a { text-decoration: underline; }` or add `border-bottom: 1px solid var(--colour-neutral-400);` at rest.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative — footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links have `text-decoration: none` and rely only on colour (neutral-400 changing to neutral-0 on hover) without an additional non-colour visual indicator such as underline, border, or font-weight change, making them indistinguishable from non-link text by colour alone.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1093: `footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { text-decoration: underline; }` or add `border-bottom: 1px solid transparent;` and `border-bottom-color: var(--colour-neutral-400);` on hover/focus.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size for .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets an explicit min-height of 36px, which falls below the 44px recommended target size for pointer inputs. While this meets the minimum 24px requirement, users with motor impairments may find the button difficult to activate reliably without enlarging the touch area.
issuesFound.fix: Line 698: Increase min-height to 44px or add at least 4px vertical padding to achieve an effective 44px target: `min-height: 44px;` or `padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-3-5);`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size for search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"] selector sets explicit width and height of 28px, creating a touch target of only 28×28px. This is below the 44px recommended size and may be difficult for users with tremors or limited dexterity to activate.
issuesFound.fix: Line 914: Increase dimensions to 44px: `width: 44px; height: 44px;`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size for checkbox input
issuesFound.explanation: The .form-group input[type="checkbox"] selector sets explicit width and height of 20px with min-height: 20px, creating a touch target below the 24px minimum required by WCAG 2.5.8. Users on touch devices may struggle to toggle the control accurately.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2933: Increase size to minimum 24px: `width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px;`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size for footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: The footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a selector sets min-height: 24px, which meets the absolute minimum but falls short of the 44px recommended target size. The inline-block display without additional padding results in a small activation area.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3087: Increase min-height to 44px or add vertical padding: `min-height: 44px;` or `padding-block: var(--space-2);`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast for logo bar label
issuesFound.explanation: The .logo-bar-label uses color var(--colour-neutral-400) (#94a3b8) against background var(--colour-neutral-50) (#f8fafc), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.45:1. This fails WCAG AA requirements for normal text (4.5:1), making the label difficult to read for users with low vision.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast for footer address text
issuesFound.explanation: The .footer-brand address uses color var(--colour-neutral-500) (#64748b) against background var(--colour-neutral-900) (#0f172a), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 3.84:1. issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: foreground #94a3b8 / background #ffffff ratio 2.5:1
issuesFound.explanation:
The .footer-brand > p element uses var(--colour-neutral-400) which resolves to #94a3b8 against a dark background? No, against white (footer background is neutral-900? No, footer background is neutral-900 at line 1430, but the text color is neutral-400.
   Wait, line 1430: `footer[role="contentinfo"] { background-color: var(--colour-neutral-900); ... }`
   Line 1436: `.footer-brand > p { ... color: var(--colour-neutral-400); ... }`
   #94a3b8 on #0f172a (neutral-900).
   #0f172a is very dark (15,23,42).
   Contrast of #94a3b8 on #0f172a:
   L1 (light) = 0.4 (approx for #94a3b8)
   L2 (dark) = 0.05 (approx for #0f172a)
   (0.4+0.05)/(0.05+0.05) = 0.45/0.1 = 4.5:1. This actually passes AA for normal text (4.5:1).
   Let me calculate properly.
   #0f172a: R=15/255=0.0588, G=23/255=0.0902, B=42/255=0.1647.
   R_corr=(0.1138/1.055)^2.4=0.108^2.4=0.0117*0.69=0.008.
   G_corr=(0.1452/1.055)^2.4=0.138^2.4=0.019*0.72=0.014.
   B_corr=(0.2197/1.055)^2.4=0.208^2.4=0.043*0.77=0.033.
   L=0.2126*0.008+0.7152*0.014+0.0722*0.033=0.0017+0.010+0.0024=0.014.
   Contrast = (0.4019+0.05)/(0.014+0.05) = 0.4519/0.064 = 7.06:1. This passes.
   
   So I should not report line 1436.

issuesFound.fix: Line 394: .btn-icon { min-width: 24px; min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-2-5); border-radius: var(--radius-full); aspect-ratio: 1; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on search button

issuesFound.fix: Line 493: #site-search button[type="submit"] { position: absolute; right: var(--space-3); display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 28px; height: 28px; padding: var(--space-2); background: none; border: none; cursor: pointer; color: var(--colour-neutral-500); border-radius: var(--radius-md); transition: color var(--transition-fast); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on checkboxes

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: The footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a selector sets min-height: 24px without accompanying padding to reach the 44px recommended touch target size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 807: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-400); text-decoration: none; transition: color var(--transition-fast); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); display: inline-block; min-height: 44px; padding-block: var(--space-2); }

issuesFound.title: Broken visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, overflow: visible, and clip: auto, which makes the content visible instead of hiding it visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers. It also fails to use the standard clip-path or rect clipping technique.
issuesFound.fix: Line 300: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on hero CTA button
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-ctas .btn-primary selector includes transform in its transition, which can trigger motion sensitivity. No @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override exists for this specific selector.
issuesFound.fix: Line 534: (add after the .hero-ctas .btn-primary rule block) @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces the space between words and can make text difficult to read for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 123: body { min-height: 100%; font-family: var(--font-family-sans); font-size: var(--font-size-base); font-weight: var(--font-weight-normal); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); color: var(--colour-text-primary); background-color: var(--colour-bg-page); -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased; -moz-osx-font-smoothing: grayscale; text-rendering: optimizeLegibility; overflow-x: hidden; position: relative; word-spacing: normal; letter-spacing: -0.05em; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and only provides outline-offset, leaving no visible focus indicator for keyboard users. This removes the default browser focus ring without replacing it, violating WCAG 2.4.11.
issuesFound.fix: Line 332: :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.fix: Line 343: :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid ButtonText; outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Broken visually-hidden utility class
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static and width: auto, making content visible instead of hiding it visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers. This defeats the purpose of the class.
issuesFound.fix: Line 362: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property, but there is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this spatial movement for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 424: (add to existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) at line 323 or create new block) @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image includes transform in its transition and has a hover transform effect, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 823 is empty, leaving the animation active for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 823: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces space between words and can impair readability for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 316: body { min-height: 100%; font-family: var(--font-family-sans); font-size: var(--font-size-base); font-weight: var(--font-weight-normal); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); color: var(--colour-text-primary); background-color: var(--colour-bg-page); -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased; -moz-osx-font-smoothing: grayscale; text-rendering: optimizeLegibility; overflow-x: hidden; position: relative; word-spacing: normal; letter-spacing: -0.05em; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible pseudo-class sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px as a replacement. Outline-offset alone does not create a visible focus ring, leaving keyboard users without any indication of focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 346: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and width: auto, which keeps the content visible in the layout and does not hide it visually. This utility class fails to properly hide content visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 384: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The button class includes transform in its transition property, which can cause motion animation. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this transform for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Add to existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 461: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { html { scroll-behavior: auto; } .btn { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image has a transition on transform properties that creates motion effects. The existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 925 is empty and does not override this animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 925: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: The body element has word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces the space between words and can make text difficult to read for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 323: body { min-height: 100%; font-family: var(--font-family-sans); font-size: var(--font-size-base); font-weight: var(--font-weight-normal); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); color: var(--colour-text-primary); background-color: var(--colour-bg-page); -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased; -moz-osx-font-smoothing: grayscale; text-rendering: optimizeLegibility; overflow-x: hidden; position: relative; word-spacing: normal; letter-spacing: -0.05em; }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector sets text-decoration: none without providing an alternative non-colour visual differentiator (such as underline, border-bottom, or font-weight change) in the same rule. This causes links to be distinguished only by colour, which fails WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color.
issuesFound.fix: Line 267: a { text-decoration: underline; } OR a { text-decoration: none; border-bottom: 1px solid currentColor; }

issuesFound.title: File upload validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When a file fails validation (size or type constraints), the fileUploadField module announces the error via liveRegion and displays it in the file list, but it does not set aria-invalid="true" on the file input element itself. Screen reader users navigating back to the input will not be informed that it is in an error state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4230: Add `setAttr(binding.inputEl, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` after the line `liveRegion.announceAssertive(`${file.name}: ${validation.error}`);` and before the `return;`. Also add code to clear aria-invalid when files are cleared or validation passes.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu state changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu module opens and closes panels when users interact with trigger buttons, and it correctly updates aria-expanded and manages focus. However, unlike other disclosure widgets in this codebase (mobileNav, faqAccordion), it does not announce the "opened" or "closed" state change via the liveRegion utility. Users relying on screen readers may not perceive that the menu has appeared or disappeared unless they notice the focus move.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2818 (after emit in _openInstance): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');`. Line 2842 (after emit in _closeInstance): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');`.

fpTitles: File upload validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
Dropdown menu state changes not announced to screen readers

issuesFound.title: Wizard progressbar missing aria-valuemin and aria-valuemax
issuesFound.explanation: The progressbar element `[data-wizard-progress]` receives `aria-valuenow` updates but never initializes `aria-valuemin` and `aria-valuemax`. Screen readers cannot calculate the percentage completion because the value range is undefined.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Inside `multiStepWizard.init`, after `_progressBar = qs('[data-wizard-progress]', _form);` add: `if (_progressBar) { _progressBar.setAttribute('aria-valuemin', '0'); _progressBar.setAttribute('aria-valuemax', '100'); }`

issuesFound.title: Character counter updates not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The character counter updates its text content as the user types, but the change is not exposed to assistive technologies. Users with visual impairments cannot perceive when they are approaching the character limit.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Inside `characterCounter.attach`, after creating `counterEl`, add: `counterEl.setAttribute('aria-live', 'polite');` and ensure the text update in `_update` announces critical thresholds (e.g., when remaining < 20): `if (remaining <= 20) liveRegion.announce(`${remaining} characters remaining`);`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer missing aria-hidden when closed
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is closed, the script removes CSS classes but does not set `aria-hidden="true"`. If the CSS uses off-screen positioning (transform: translateX) instead of display:none, the drawer remains in the accessibility tree and can be reached by screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In `mobileNav.close`, after `removeClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);` add: `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` and in `mobileNav.open`, after `addClass(_drawer, ...)` add: `removeAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden');`

issuesFound.title: Newsletter email input missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The email input field in the newsletter form lacks an autocomplete attribute. Browser autofill and password managers cannot identify the field's purpose, and users with cognitive disabilities may benefit from explicit field identification.
issuesFound.fix: Line: After `_emailEl = qs('input[type="email"]', _formEl);` add: `if (_emailEl) setAttr(_emailEl, 'autocomplete', 'email');`

issuesFound.title: Contact form name input missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The full name input in the contact form lacks autocomplete="name". This prevents browsers from autofill assistance and makes form completion harder for users with motor or cognitive impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line: After `_formEl` is found, add: `const nameField = qs('[name="full_name"]', _formEl); if (nameField) setAttr(nameField, 'autocomplete', 'name');`

issuesFound.title: Contact form email input missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The email input in the contact form lacks autocomplete="email", preventing proper autofill support and failing WCAG 1.3.5 Identify Input Purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line: After `_formEl` is found, add: `const emailField = qs('[name="email"]', _formEl); if (emailField) setAttr(emailField, 'autocomplete', 'email');`

issuesFound.title: Scroll-to-top button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The scroll-to-top button is selected from the DOM but the script does not ensure it has an accessible name (aria-label). If the HTML lacks visible text or a label, screen reader users will hear only "button" without context.
issuesFound.fix: Line: After `_btn = qs(SELECTORS.scrollTopBtn);` and the null check, add: `if (!_btn.getAttribute('aria-label')) setAttr(_btn, 'aria-label', STRINGS.labels.scrollToTop || 'Scroll back to top');`

issuesFound.title: Site search input missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The search input is enhanced with ARIA roles (combobox) but the script does not verify or provide an accessible name. Without a label or aria-label, screen reader users cannot identify the input's purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line: After `_inputEl = qs(cfg.inputSelector);` and the null check, add: `if (_inputEl && !_inputEl.getAttribute('aria-label') && !_inputEl.getAttribute('aria-labelledby')) setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-label', STRINGS.labels.search || 'Search');`

issuesFound.title: Stat counter value changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When statistic counters animate to their final values, the change is not announced to screen reader users. Users may miss important numerical updates that are visually prominent but not communicated programmatically.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Inside the `onComplete` callback of `animateNumber` in `_animate`, after `el.setAttribute('aria-label', el.textContent);` add: `liveRegion.announce(el.textContent + ' ' + (el.getAttribute('data-stat-label') || 'statistic updated'));`

This prevents browsers from autofill assistance for personal name data, failing WCAG 2.2 Success Criterion 1.3.5 (Identify Input Purpose).

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open function displays the navigation drawer by adding CSS classes but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen readers cannot convey the expanded state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); immediately after the line _open = true;

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance function reveals the dropdown panel but fails to update aria-expanded to "true" on the trigger element, leaving assistive technology users unaware the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); immediately after the line inst.open = true;

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The open function expands the FAQ answer panel and updates internal state, but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button, preventing screen readers from announcing the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); immediately after the line item.open = true;

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The close function hides the mobile navigation drawer but provides no auditory feedback via a live region, so screen reader users are not notified that the menu has closed.
issuesFound.fix: Add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed'); immediately before emitting the 'mobilenav:close' event.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Opening a dropdown menu updates the visual UI but does not announce the state change via an aria-live region, leaving screen reader users unaware the menu has appeared.
issuesFound.fix: Add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Dropdown menu opened'); immediately after opening the panel.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The _closeInstance function collapses the dropdown panel without notifying assistive technologies via a live region, potentially leaving users unaware the menu has closed.
issuesFound.fix: Add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Dropdown menu closed'); immediately before emitting the 'dropdown:close' event.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.close missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Collapsing an FAQ answer updates the visual state but does not announce the change to screen readers via the live region system.
issuesFound.fix: Add liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqClosed || 'Answer collapsed'); immediately before emitting the 'faq:close' event.

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator._renderResult missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: When validation fails, the function marks the field invalid visually and links the error message, but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the input element, so screen readers cannot convey the invalid state.
issuesFound.fix: In the else branch (when result.valid is false), add setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true'); immediately before or after the call to srUtils.markInvalid(field, errorEl);

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open missing focus move to drawer
issuesFound.explanation: The function opens the navigation drawer but does not move keyboard focus into the drawer content, leaving keyboard users stranded at the trigger button instead of being able to navigate the menu.
issuesFound.fix: Add const firstFocusable = getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0] || _drawer; firstFocusable.focus({ preventScroll: true }); immediately after opening the drawer and before emitting the open event.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close missing focus return to trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The function closes the drawer but does not return keyboard focus to the trigger button, disorienting keyboard users who expect focus to return to the element that opened the dialog.
issuesFound.fix: Add _trigger.focus({ preventScroll: true }); at the end of the function, after unlocking scroll and before emitting the close event.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing focus trap activation
issuesFound.explanation: The module declares a focus trap variable (_trap) but never instantiates or activates it, allowing focus to escape the drawer when open and failing to return focus to the trigger on close.
issuesFound.fix: In open(), add: if (!_trap) { _trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger }); } _trap.activate(); Ensure _trap.deactivate() remains in close().

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open does not set aria-expanded="true" on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The open function toggles the navigation drawer visibility and CSS classes but fails to update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to "true". Screen readers will continue to announce the button as "collapsed" even after the menu opens, violating WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1842: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the line `_open = true;`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid helper adds an error message via aria-describedby but never sets aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Assistive technologies cannot programmatically determine that the field contains an error, violating WCAG 4.1.2 and 3.3.1 Error Identification.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1202: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` as the first line inside the markInvalid function body.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open function fails to activate focus trap
issuesFound.explanation: The module declares a _trap variable and references it in the close function, but the open function never initializes or activates a focus trap for the modal navigation drawer. Keyboard users can tab out of the drawer into the background content, violating WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order and 2.4.7 Focus Visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1848: Add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: true }); _trap.activate();` after the drawer is made visible.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close function fails to return focus to trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When the navigation drawer closes, focus is not programmatically returned to the hamburger trigger button. If focus was inside the drawer, it is lost to the document body, violating WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1885: Add `_trigger.focus();` before the final emit call in the close function.

issuesFound.title: fileUploadField._addFile does not set aria-invalid on validation failure
issuesFound.explanation: When a selected file fails validation (size or type), the function announces the error via liveRegion but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the file input element. Screen readers cannot identify the field as having an error.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4070: Add `inputEl.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` inside the if (!validation.valid) block before returning.

issuesFound.title: discountCodeInput._onApply does not set aria-invalid on validation failure
issuesFound.explanation: When the discount code validation fails, the function updates the status message and announces it, but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the discount code input field (_inputEl).
issuesFound.fix: Line 2970: Add `_inputEl.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` inside the else block (when result.valid is false) before calling liveRegion.announceAssertive.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function in mobileNav toggles the navigation drawer visibility but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen readers cannot announce the expanded state, leaving users unaware the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1160: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `_open = true;` inside the open() function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance() function reveals dropdown panels but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This violates the disclosure pattern, preventing assistive technologies from announcing the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1255: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `inst.open = true;` inside _openInstance().

issuesFound.title: productFilter._syncTabAriaStates() missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons use the aria-pressed pattern per the HTML contract, but the function only toggles CSS classes without setting aria-pressed to reflect the active state. Screen readers cannot convey which filter is active.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2404: Add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` inside the forEach loop after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() not setting aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid() utility adds error descriptions via aria-describedby but never sets aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen readers cannot announce the invalid state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~802: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` inside markInvalid() after addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`.

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator not setting aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The _renderResult() function calls srUtils.markInvalid() when validation fails, but markInvalid() does not set aria-invalid. Consequently, invalid fields lack the aria-invalid attribute required by screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2608: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` inside the else block (invalid result) of _renderResult(), or modify srUtils.markInvalid() as noted in Issue 5.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.close() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The close() function collapses FAQ panels but provides no live region announcement when announceToSR is true (the parameter is ignored). Screen reader users receive no confirmation that content was collapsed.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The close() function accepts an announceToSR parameter but never uses it to announce the closed state. Users navigating with screen readers are not informed when the mobile menu closes.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The _closeInstance() function hides dropdown panels without announcing the collapsed state via the live region. Screen reader users may not realize the menu has closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1295: Add `liveRegion.announce('Dropdown closed');` (or appropriate string) after `if (returnFocus) inst.trigger.focus();` and before the emit() call.

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared in inlineValidator valid state
issuesFound.explanation: While srUtils.markValid() removes aria-invalid, the _renderResult() function in inlineValidator calls markValid() but also explicitly adds CSS classes. If markValid() is bypassed or the field was previously invalid, aria-invalid might persist. Ensure explicit removal.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2597: Add `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');` inside the valid result block of _renderResult(), or confirm srUtils.markValid() is always called and correctly removes the attribute.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.init does not initialize aria-expanded on trigger

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close missing aria-expanded update

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid missing aria-invalid reset

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters missing live region announcement

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger missing aria-expanded update on open

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion missing live region announcements
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing FAQ items changes visible content but does not announce the state change to screen readers via the liveRegion announcer.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2660: After `emit(_containerEl, 'faq:open', { item });` add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqOpened(item.title));`
  Line 2680: After `emit(_containerEl, 'faq:close', { item });` add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqClosed(item.title));`

issuesFound.title: Site search combobox missing aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The site search input acts as a combobox but does not toggle aria-expanded when the suggestion list opens and closes, preventing screen readers from knowing when suggestions are available.

  Line 1930: In _close function after `_searchState.open = false;` add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Site search combobox missing initial aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The search input is initialised with role="combobox" and aria-haspopup="listbox" but lacks aria-expanded="false", so screen readers assume the list is initially expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1970: After `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-haspopup', 'listbox');` add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle buttons missing aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (monthly/annual) indicate active state via CSS classes but do not set aria-pressed, so screen readers cannot identify which period is selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2480: In _syncToggleState function after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);` add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));`

issuesFound.title: Pricing module missing live region announcements for price changes
issuesFound.explanation: Switching billing periods dynamically updates price values visible on screen but does not announce the change or the new prices to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2490: In setPeriod function after `_syncToggleState(period);` add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.pricingMonthly);`

issuesFound.title: Product filter missing live region announcement for results
issuesFound.explanation: Applying filters updates the product grid and count badge but does not announce the filter results (e.g., "Showing 5 products") to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2220: In applyFilters callback after `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);` add `liveRegion.announce(totalMatches === 0 ? STRINGS.filterAll : STRINGS.filterApplied(s.activeCategory));`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid not setting aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid utility function links error messages via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field, so screen readers do not identify the field as invalid.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid not clearing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid utility function removes error descriptions but does not clear the aria-invalid attribute (or set it to false), leaving the field marked as invalid for screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1390: At the end of markValid function add `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');` or `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Inline validator not reflecting validation state in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator _renderResult function relies on srUtils.markInvalid and markValid, which do not handle aria-invalid. Consequently, form fields never receive aria-invalid attributes when validation errors occur or are cleared.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3420: In _renderResult when result.valid is false, after `srUtils.markInvalid(field, errorEl);` add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`
  Line 3430: In _renderResult when result.valid is true, after `srUtils.markValid(field, errorEl);` add `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown panel is shown by removing the hidden attribute and adding CSS classes, but the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute is never set to "true" to indicate the expanded state to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` immediately after `inst.open = true;` inside _openInstance.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _closeInstance missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown panel is hidden by adding the hidden attribute and removing CSS classes, but the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute is never set to "false" to indicate the collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` immediately after `inst.open = false;` inside _closeInstance.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch _open missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The search suggestion listbox is displayed, but the combobox input element does not have its aria-expanded attribute set to "true", failing to communicate the expanded state to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` inside the _open function after setting `_searchState.open = true;`.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch _close missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The search suggestion listbox is hidden, but the combobox input element does not have its aria-expanded attribute set to "false", leaving screen readers unaware that the popup has collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` inside the _close function.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: While the close function sets aria-expanded="false", the open function fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the accordion trigger button. This asymmetry prevents screen readers from announcing when an FAQ item expands.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` immediately after `item.open = true;` inside the open function.

issuesFound.title: pricing _syncToggleState missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons indicate selection via CSS classes only. They do not update the aria-pressed attribute to communicate the pressed state to assistive technology users.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Inside the forEach loop over _periodBtns, add `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, isActive);` after determining isActive.

issuesFound.title: productFilter _syncTabAriaStates missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons indicate active state via CSS class toggling only. They lack aria-pressed (or aria-selected) attributes to communicate which filter is currently active to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Inside the forEach loop over filter tabs, add `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, isActive);` or `setAttr(btn, ARIA.SELECTED, isActive);` after determining isActive.

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator _renderResult missing aria-invalid on invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When a field fails validation, the function adds error classes and descriptions but never sets aria-invalid="true" on the field element, so screen readers do not announce the invalid state.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In the else branch (invalid case), add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, true);` alongside `addClass(field, CSS.fieldInvalid);`.

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator _renderResult missing aria-invalid clear on valid
issuesFound.explanation: When a field passes validation, the function removes error styling but does not set aria-invalid="false" or remove the attribute, potentially leaving stale invalid states announced to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In the if branch (valid case), add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, false);` or `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);` alongside `removeClass(field, CSS.fieldInvalid);`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav init missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button is initialized without aria-expanded="false", violating the requirement that ARIA states reflect the initial closed state of the disclosure widget on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line: After `_trigger = qs(SELECTORS.navToggle);` and checking existence, add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu register missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons are initialized with aria-haspopup and aria-controls, but aria-expanded is not set to "false" initially to indicate the closed state on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line: After setting aria-controls, add `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`.

issuesFound.title: suggestionListRenderer mount missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The search input is configured as a combobox with aria-controls and aria-haspopup, but lacks aria-expanded="false" initialization, leaving screen readers unaware of the collapsed initial state.
issuesFound.fix: Line: After `setAttr(input, 'aria-haspopup', 'listbox');`, add `setAttr(input, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close missing aria-hidden on drawer
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation drawer is hidden by removing CSS classes only, without setting aria-hidden="true". If the CSS does not explicitly set display:none, the drawer remains in the accessibility tree when visually hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add `setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, true);` after `removeClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open missing aria-hidden removal on drawer
issuesFound.explanation: When opening the drawer, if aria-hidden was previously set to hide it from the accessibility tree, the function does not remove aria-hidden to make the drawer accessible again.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add `removeAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN);` or `setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, false);` after `addClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing focus trap activation
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer acts as a modal dialog blocking the rest of the page, but the declared _trap variable is never instantiated or activated. Keyboard users can tab out of the drawer into the background page.
issuesFound.fix: Line: After opening the drawer and setting attributes, add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger }); _trap.activate();`.

issuesFound.title: toastManager auto-dismiss without pause mechanism
issuesFound.explanation: Toast notifications automatically dismiss after a timeout using setTimeout, but there is no mechanism to pause, stop, or hide this auto-updating content on hover or focus, violating WCAG 2.2.1.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Replace simple setTimeout with pausable logic: `let timerId = setTimeout(() => dismiss(toast), dur); toast.addEventListener('mouseenter', () => clearTimeout(timerId)); toast.addEventListener('mouseleave', () => timerId = setTimeout(() => dismiss(toast), dur));`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav state change not announced via live region

issuesFound.fix: Line in open(): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');`. Line in close(): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing dropdown menus changes the visible content, but no live region announcement informs screen reader users that the menu has appeared or disappeared.
issuesFound.fix: Line in _openInstance: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');`. Line in _closeInstance: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');`.

issuesFound.title: pricing period change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Switching between monthly and annual billing periods updates prices visually but does not announce the change or the new prices to screen reader users via a live region.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly);` after updating the UI.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion state change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding or collapsing FAQ items changes the visible content height, but no live region announcement informs screen reader users of the state change or the answer content.
issuesFound.fix: Line in open: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqOpened ? STRINGS.announce.faqOpened(item.question) : \`${item.question} expanded\`);`. Line in close: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqClosed ? STRINGS.announce.faqClosed(item.question) : \`${item.question} collapsed\`);`.

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email input broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput element references 'newsletter-email-error' in its aria-describedby attribute when validation fails, but the parent FormGroup component is not provided with the errorId prop required to render the ErrorMessage element with that ID. Additionally, FormGroup expects an errorMessage prop, but NewsletterForm passes error. This results in a broken ARIA reference that prevents screen readers from associating the error message with the input field.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1179: Change `error={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}` to `errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}` and add `errorId="newsletter-email-error"` to the FormGroup props.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm email input broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput references 'contact-email-error' in aria-describedby when validation fails, but FormGroup lacks the errorId prop. FormGroup expects errorMessage but receives error.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm subject input broken aria-describedby reference

issuesFound.title: ContactForm message textarea broken aria-describedby reference

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm submit button broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The Button has aria-describedby="newsletter-status" permanently set, but the element with this ID only exists when the form status is 'success' (line 1167) or 'error' (line 1196). When the form is idle or submitting, screen readers reference a non-existent element.

issuesFound.title: Modal dialog missing aria-modal attribute

issuesFound.fix: Line 1369: Remove `aria-modal="true"` from the modal-overlay div. Line 1370: Add `aria-modal="true"` to the div with `role="dialog"`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion disclosure button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The accordion trigger button in FaqItemComponent manages an open/closed state and has aria-controls, but lacks aria-expanded. Assistive technology cannot announce the current expanded/collapsed state of the FAQ panel.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1676: <button type="button" id={btnId} aria-controls={panelId} aria-expanded={isOpen} className="faq-item__trigger" onClick={() => onToggle(item.id)}>

issuesFound.title: TextInput component fails to map invalid prop to aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component accepts an `invalid` boolean prop but does not set the `aria-invalid` attribute on the underlying <input>. Screen readers cannot convey the error state to users when validation fails.
issuesFound.fix: Line 778: <input id={id} aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} className={['form-input', invalid ? 'form-input--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest} />

issuesFound.title: ContactForm name field references non-existent error element
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput for "contact-name" sets aria-describedby="contact-name-error" when an error exists, but FormGroup is never passed the errorId prop, so no element with that ID is rendered. This creates a broken ARIA reference.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1840: <FormGroup label="Full name" htmlFor="contact-name" required errorId="contact-name-error" errorMessage={name.state.error}> AND remove aria-describedby from the TextInput on line 1856 (FormGroup will handle it).

issuesFound.title: ContactForm email field references non-existent error element
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput for "contact-email" sets aria-describedby="contact-email-error" when an error exists, but FormGroup is never passed the errorId prop, so the referenced element is absent from the DOM.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1860: <FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="contact-email" required errorId="contact-email-error" errorMessage={email.state.error}> AND remove aria-describedby from the TextInput on line 1876.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm subject field references non-existent error element
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput for "contact-subject" sets aria-describedby="contact-subject-error" when an error exists, but FormGroup is never passed the errorId prop, so the referenced element is absent from the DOM.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1880: <FormGroup label="Subject" htmlFor="contact-subject" required errorId="contact-subject-error" errorMessage={subject.state.error}> AND remove aria-describedby from the TextInput on line 1896.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm message field references non-existent error element
issuesFound.explanation: The Textarea for "contact-message" includes "contact-message-error" in aria-describedby when an error exists, but FormGroup is never passed the errorId prop, so the referenced element is absent from the DOM.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1900: <FormGroup label="Message" htmlFor="contact-message" required errorId="contact-message-error" errorMessage={message.state.error}> AND update aria-describedby on line 1920 to remove the conditional error reference (FormGroup will handle it).

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email field references non-existent error element

issuesFound.fix:
Line 1480: <button type="button" id="billing-monthly" aria-pressed={value === 'monthly'} className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'monthly' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={() => onChange('monthly')}>

issuesFound.title: Modal aria-modal incorrectly placed on overlay
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal attribute is placed on the modal overlay div instead of the dialog element with role="dialog". Screen readers may not correctly identify the modal as modal, potentially allowing users to navigate outside the dialog while it is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1202: <div className="modal-overlay" onClick={onClose}> Line 1205: <div ref={dialogRef} role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby={titleId} aria-describedby={description ? descId : undefined} className={['modal-dialog', `modal-dialog--${size}`].join(' ')} onClick={(ev) => ev.stopPropagation()}>

issuesFound.title: PlanCard article missing aria-labelledby
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing plan card is an article element that contains a heading but lacks aria-labelledby to associate the heading with the article, making it difficult for screen reader users to identify the card's purpose when navigating by region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1470: <article className="pricing-card__article" aria-labelledby={headingId}>

issuesFound.title: IntegrationsBar cloned list missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The second list of integration logos is a visual clone for seamless looping animation but is not hidden from assistive technology, causing duplicate content to be announced.

issuesFound.title: StarRating component missing role="img"
issuesFound.explanation: The star rating widget displays visual stars but lacks role="img", so screen readers may not treat it as a single image with a text alternative.
issuesFound.fix: Line 254: <span role="img" aria-label={`${labelPrefix}: ${value} out of ${max} stars`}>

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown chevron not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown chevron arrow is decorative text content inside a button that already has a text label, but it is not hidden from assistive technology and may be announced as "black up-pointing triangle" or similar.
issuesFound.fix: Line 598: <span className="nav-link__chevron" aria-hidden="true">

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm FormGroup missing errorId for aria-describedby
